questions.question: Explain the difference between a component and a container in Swing.
keyPoints: Component: Basic UI element that can be displayed and interact with users
Container: Special component that can hold other components
All containers are components, but not all components are containers
Containers manage layout of child components

questions.question: Describe the component hierarchy in a typical Swing application and explain the role of layout managers in container-component relationships.
keyPoints: Top-level containers (JFrame, JDialog) form the foundation
Content pane holds visible components in top-level containers
Layout managers control positioning and sizing of components
Different layout managers for different organizational needs
Importance of proper hierarchy for maintainable code
